fields.description:
## Why
Bin-packing contamination is currently UNMEASURABLE: the sidecar stamps each finding's criteria but throws away chunk co-residency (only a chunk COUNT survives). Cannot calibrate the R-1-part-2 tradeoff (epic fort-wisp-wren) without measuring it.

## What (corrected code targets)
- Stamp each emitted Pass-1 finding with its chunk COHORT (sorted criterion-id set). CORRECTION: findings are emitted by THREE finder paths, not one: pass1_chunk (passes.py def@255), pass1_container (def@310), pass1_isf (def@409). Stamp all three or the sidecar is silently partial (container/ISF findings would lack cohort).
- Carry the key through _slim (sidecar.py def@228) -- its return dict is an explicit key-ALLOWLIST, so cohort must be added there or it is stripped.
- Out-of-plan_review consumers of the finding shape must be checked: src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py (~line 100) and src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py (~line 365) both call pass1_chunk.
- Document the new sidecar field's semantics (schema note / CLAUDE.md) since offline tooling consumes it.

## Provenance
Labels like G-8 / FP-3a / R-6 / WS-N are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020) -- they are NOT codebase symbols and cannot be grepped. Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cohort (sorted chunk criterion-id set) stamped on findings from pass1_chunk AND pass1_container AND pass1_isf, and present in _slim's allowlist. Proof: run a minimal review; `jq '.findings[].cohort' <sidecar>` non-null for all.
- [ ] Sidecar schema note added for the cohort field.
- [ ] Offline analysis can compute a blocking-tier contamination rate (finding-on-A co-resided with {B,C}). Proof: a small script/query over sidecar corpus prints the rate; check the two out-of-tree callers still pass. `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py`.
- [ ] Unblocks fort-wisp-wren (R-1 part 2).
